texto: We find people of value,' she said, speaking like a teacher addressing a small child, 'and we determine what story they need to hear. It's the story that they're already telling themselves, don't you see? It's the nightmare they believe in. Once you understand that nightmare, you join them in it. Their fear becomes your fear. It's all a shared experience then. And once you have that, once they feel that is the truth, all the way down to their core, then your coping strategy becomes theirs. It's a natural progression. This is the power of a shared narrative. Of the echo chamber.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Koryta: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “We find people of value,' she said, speaking like a teacher addressing a small child, 'and we det…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, alt-right, psychopaths pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

texto: He stood at the edge of town feeling very small, powerless. Night in the mountains could do that to you, reminding you of your place in the world and laughing at any sense of self-importance.
explicacao: Citação de Michael Koryta sobre temas ligados a humor. “He stood at the edge of town feeling very small, powerless. Night in the mountains could do that …” articula humor, mountains, nature em registro de humor, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
